last_item: Login — the sign-in form was a PERMANENT DEAD-END after a server lockout. On repeated wrong admin passwords auth.Authenticate returns CodeLocked (HTTP 429); the handler set locked=true (input disabled={busy||locked}) and cleared the password (button disabled={busy||!password}), and locked only resets inside onSubmit (setLocked(false), line 38) — which could never fire because both controls were disabled. So the form froze with no recovery but a full-page reload (which a user has no reason to discover), and it stayed frozen even after the server lock window expired. Distinct from the parked 'Try again later' copy nit — a state-machine dead-end, not wording. Surfaced by the iter-21 Settings/Login convergence agent (the other 4 of 5 agents converged); verified against the code before promoting — including that the existing Login test literally ENCODED the dead-end (asserted input.toBeDisabled() after lockout).
last_result: SHIPPED (4 SHIP, zero blockers — incl. restraint critic). Subtractive fix: input disabled={busy || locked} -> disabled={busy} (removed the lockout-disable; added a 5-line comment explaining why). The lockout is still surfaced by the existing warn-tone Callout, and the SERVER remains the sole enforcer of the lock — the UI disable was never the enforcement, only the trap. A locked-out user can now retype + resubmit; the resubmit clears locked and the server re-decides (still locked -> warn again; expired -> logs in). Zero added UI/controls/copy. Updated the test that encoded the dead-end ('...disables the input') to assert the lockout is still surfaced (warn alert + cleared field) AND the input stays editable AND a retype+resubmit fires a second auth attempt (recovery without reload). Panel: UX heuristics ('absolute User-Control-&-Freedom violation: a permanently unrecoverable state with no visible escape; one-char removal, no weakened security boundary; H#4 consistency win — lockout now clears+re-enables like the wrong-password path'); Sam ('I'd have sat there reloading like an idiot; now the box stays alive, I retype and I'm in — no docs'); Priya ('removes a wall, keeps the gate where it belongs — on the server; nothing buried, nothing slowed'); restraint critic ('the rare change a simplicity critic should wave through — makes the code smaller and removes a trap; do-nothing leaves every locked-out admin frozen until they guess reload'). Both personas re-flagged the lockout-duration copy as non-blocking polish (the parked low/watch item). Gates: typecheck OK, 145 tests OK (updated 1 existing case, no net new), build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (exit 0, outside sandbox). stable_streak stays 0 (shipped a real improvement — convergence clock restarts). LESSON: a disabled state with no path to re-enable itself is a dead-end, not a safeguard — trace every guard flag to 'what re-enables this, and can the user reach it?'; if the only answer is 'reload the page', it's a defect, and removing the disable is a subtractive fix the restraint critic waves through. Watch for tests that ENCODE the bug (the green Login test asserted the dead-end). Backlog is actionable-empty again (NEEDS-BACKEND item + the parked low/watch Login copy nit + Query write-detector watch item). Next iteration: run a fresh Mode-S discovery/convergence pass.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
